purpose: Quality-first top recipe blueprints for family-wide recipe authoring
authoringRules: Author top recipes before long-tail variants.
Every recipe must improve hierarchy, scan rhythm, and page-role clarity, not just surface styling.
Reject filler recipes that only change color or decoration without structural advantage.
Manual render ceiling should be checked before expanding recipe count.
If top recipes still fail after contract and critic alignment, stop and run renderer surface compare.

families.selectionRule: Treat hero as the strongest top-stage surface. The first glance must already show dominance.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Create a first-scroll command surface where the visual and headline clearly overpower the support rail.
structureRules: Lead visual should own at least half of the horizontal stage.
Headline block should read before any support card or badge cluster.
Support rail should feel secondary and editorial, not equal-width promo cards.
copyRules: Headline should land in one dominant phrase before descriptive copy starts.
Primary CTA must be visually louder than any secondary action.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Lift premium perception without losing product-entry clarity.
structureRules: Lead copy stack should be shorter and more forceful than the utility support zone.
Premium surface must still preserve a direct path to the main action.
Support cards should reinforce curation, not compete with the lead card.
copyRules: Use premium but concrete benefit language.
Keep the lead message compressed; avoid generic lifestyle filler.
qualitySignals: Headline dominance survives at first scroll
Premium tone comes from contrast and spacing, not decoration only

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Offer a softer editorial top stage while preserving strong visual entry hierarchy.
structureRules: Lead column should still dominate even when the tone is editorial.
Support content must read as chapter-like continuation, not equal promo tiles.
Whitespace should frame the headline instead of diluting it.
copyRules: Use one editorial hook plus one supporting line.
Do not let descriptive copy outgrow the headline block.

families.selectionRule: Treat quickmenu as a cluster under hero, not as a flat icon dump.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Create one obvious lead entry and a clean support grid that scans in under two seconds.
structureRules: Lead panel must be materially larger or higher-contrast than the rest.
Support entries should share one icon shell logic and one text rhythm.
The cluster should feel attached to hero, not like a separate tool strip.
copyRules: Labels must carry context, not bare verbs.
Support labels should stay compact enough to preserve scan speed.
qualitySignals: Consistent icon shell and card spacing

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Use the quickmenu cluster as a guided service start surface with strong information scent.
structureRules: Primary route should be visually first and semantically clearest.
Remaining entries should form a stable support matrix with obvious grouping.
Card rhythm should favor trust and legibility over visual drama.
copyRules: Each label should imply a concrete user task or service outcome.
Avoid marketing slogans in place of service utility.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Support hero with a curated ribbon that feels designed, not merely utilitarian.
structureRules: First editorial card must act as a gateway, not as one of many equal cards.
Remaining cards should maintain consistent rhythm and restrained copy length.
Strip pacing should feel horizontal and cinematic, not like a wrapped chip list.
copyRules: Use short, high-signal labels that still imply meaning.
Do not let decorative eyebrow text overpower the destination label.

families.selectionRule: Ranking must show order, not just a list of similar cards.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Make rank 1 feel decisively more important while preserving clean secondary scan.
structureRules: Rank 1 card should be larger or framed with a stronger poster treatment.
Rank markers must be visible before title parsing begins.
Secondary ranks should compress into a stable ladder without collapsing into sameness.
copyRules: Keep title and one supporting merch signal visible at first glance.
Do not bury rank signal under promotion badges.
qualitySignals: Poster-like contrast for the first item

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Use a compact ranking layout that still preserves ordered emphasis and comparison clarity.
structureRules: A clear lead column or lead row should define the entry point.
Product image, title, and rank marker must align on one predictable axis.
Comparison fields should stay visually secondary to rank and product identity.
copyRules: Prefer compact evidence such as rating, price, or tag over long descriptions.

families.selectionRule: Banner should compress a campaign idea into one readable action frame.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Deliver a single-message banner with strong visual framing and unmissable action.
structureRules: Message block should sit inside a clearly protected readable zone.
CTA must sit in the same focal region as the message, not detached at the far edge.
Image treatment must support the campaign frame, not overpower the text.
copyRules: Use one compressed campaign claim plus one action.
Avoid multiline marketing stacks that require paragraph reading.
avoid: headline spread across too many lines

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Use a calmer banner to surface one benefit or event without slowing the browsing rhythm.
structureRules: Banner should read as one compact band with one dominant idea.
Support iconography or image must only reinforce the message.
Vertical space should stay controlled so the strip does not feel heavy.
copyRules: Benefit wording should be concrete and immediately useful.
Secondary explanation must remain optional, not necessary for understanding.
qualitySignals: Low friction insertion between larger sections
avoid: poster-sized height for a simple message

families.selectionRule: Commerce recipes must improve merchandising clarity before adding tone.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Create a merch grid with one lead product and a calm, scannable secondary set.
structureRules: Lead card should carry stronger image scale and slightly deeper info density.
Secondary cards should align on identical image and text baselines.
Price or offer stack must remain legible without looking noisy.
copyRules: Prioritize product name, price, and one decision aid.
Do not stack multiple promotional badges above the title.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Blend product merchandising with editorial context without losing conversion scent.
structureRules: One featured story/product pair should anchor the section.
Secondary items should form a supporting rail rather than another equal grid.
Image aspect ratios should feel intentional and not random across cards.
copyRules: Use story framing in the headline, but keep product utility obvious.
Secondary cards should remain short and scannable.
avoid: utility grid pretending to be editorial

families.selectionRule: Story recipes must create chapter rhythm, not just card collections.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Make one lead chapter feel like the editorial entry while the rail supports continuous exploration.
structureRules: Lead story block should be materially larger or visually calmer than the rail.
Rail items should maintain equal rhythm and restrained metadata.
Whitespace should create chapter pacing instead of empty space.
copyRules: Lead story can carry a stronger narrative hook.
Support stories should stay compact and title-led.
avoid: soft editorial tone with no lead focus

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Use portrait-led story cards to convey depth and proof without slowing scan too much.
structureRules: Portrait aspect ratio should be consistent across support cards.
Headline, category cue, and image must align in a repeatable reading order.
The section should still preserve one stronger anchor card or opener.
copyRules: Use titles that suggest a concrete story payoff.
Keep supporting descriptors short and proof-like.

families.selectionRule: Benefit sections should prove value fast and support conversion with trust.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Chunk benefits into proof-led blocks that read quickly and feel credible.
structureRules: Each benefit block should pair one proof cue with one concrete outcome.
The section should keep a consistent grid or band rhythm across all blocks.
Trust cues must not be visually smaller than decorative icons.
copyRules: Benefit copy should be outcome-led, not slogan-led.
Proof or qualifier text should stay short but visible.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Combine trust, service reassurance, and the next action inside one calm conversion-support surface.
structureRules: One lead reassurance block should frame the section before smaller benefit cells.
Benefit cells should align to one icon/container system.
CTA or next step must be visible but not disruptive.
copyRules: Use reassuring language tied to an action or support outcome.
Avoid generic corporate trust claims.

families.selectionRule: Service tabs should reduce choice friction and clarify selected-state context immediately.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Create a dashboard-like service selector where the active tab and active panel are unmistakable.
structureRules: Selected tab must be visually and semantically dominant.
Panel content should begin with the most actionable information, not generic intro copy.
Tabs and panel should feel like one system, not separate layers.
copyRules: Tab labels should map to real service tasks or categories.
Panel headlines should resolve the user question immediately.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Use tab panels to surface proof, policy, or reassurance without overwhelming the service journey.
structureRules: Each panel should start with a trust or proof summary before details.
Dense information should be chunked into calm sub-blocks.
Tab bar should remain visually stable while panels swap.
copyRules: Favor structured bullets and short subheads over prose.
Proof statements should be specific and scannable.
avoid: panel content with no proof hierarchy

families.selectionRule: Page shell recipes must orchestrate clusters and background shifts across the full page, not only one section.

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Make the hero-to-quickmenu-to-next-section transition feel authored as one top-stage cluster.
structureRules: Hero and quickmenu should share spacing logic and visual tempo.
The first major transition should feel deliberate through background or gap control.
The shell should avoid stacking many visually loud sections back to back.
copyRules: Top cluster labels and cues should feel related without repeating the same phrase.
avoid: constant 80px gap with no rhythm change

topRecipes.qualityObjective: Balance hard-commerce blocks and softer story blocks so the page does not feel monotonous or noisy.
structureRules: Heavy merch sections should be followed by calmer editorial or benefit breathing rooms.
Background, gap, and card density should vary by section role.
The shell must preserve a coherent overall story from top entry to lower browsing modules.
copyRules: Section headings should clearly telegraph whether the next block is browse, story, or benefit.
qualitySignals: No long run of identical section weight
avoid: story sections with no pacing benefit
